Two skies read together, and the climb you share

The Climb · Zodi Karma

Zodi Karma is the merit your animal gathers: the daily visit, the proverbs studied, the moons checked, the matches tested. It buys nothing. It only lifts you, station by station, from Wanderer to Blessed.

  • ✦Blessed by the Gods of Zodi100,000
  • ◉Awakened10,000
  • ·Wanderer0

Six stations in all — the full ladder lives on the Karmic Board.

骨 Tasmanian Devil

0 of 13 chapters read

♏ Scorpio and 狗 Year of the Dog

Tasmanian Devil

You carry the island's whole weight in your jaw, even when the biting is what costs you most.

Zodi Animal · No. 095 / 144

In brief

Your Primal Zodiac Animal is the Tasmanian Devil: Scorpio's full-weight presence crossed with the Dog's rooted loyalty.

  • Scorpio

    Holds the full weight of what it knows and does not distribute the cost of carrying it.

  • The Dog

    Stays with its territory, its people, and its commitments without revision — loyalty as a physical fact.

  • The Tasmanian Devil

    Takes everything the island has to offer, screams at whoever arrives to compete for it, and pays the price in the same place the strength lives.

One strength, one cost: the thing you do most characteristically — the commitment, the bite, the showing up — is also the exact place where damage travels in.

Meet the Tasmanian Devil

The largest living carnivorous marsupial, whose defining strength is also the exact vector of its greatest threat.

The Tasmanian Devil (Sarcophilus harrisii) is the world's largest surviving carnivorous marsupial, found only on the island of Tasmania, with jaws among the strongest relative to body size of any living mammal and a communal feeding behavior so loud and aggressive that the first European settlers gave it its name.

Every symbolic reading below rests on these specific facts. The jaw that bites through bone. The screaming feast at the carcass. The transmissible cancer that spreads through the exact behavior that defines the species. The forty embryos, four survivors. The island with no exit. These are the biology, and the biology is what the archetype is built from.

Consider what it means to be built the same way.

6 traits below

01of six

The bone bite

Animal fact

The Tasmanian devil has one of the strongest bites relative to body size of any living mammal — capable of generating bite forces that allow it to consume bone, fur, and cartilage entirely. It leaves nothing of a carcass.

Our reading

When you commit to something, you go all the way through it. You do not leave the useful parts behind because they are difficult. Other people stop before the bone. You do not.

02of six

The screaming feast

Animal fact

Tasmanian devils are communal feeders at large carcasses, competing through loud, aggressive vocalizations and displays. This screaming behavior was the basis for the species being named "devil" by early European settlers.

Our reading

You make noise at the difficult thing. Where others handle conflict quietly or avoid it, you arrive at the carcass and announce yourself. The noise is not aggression for its own sake — it is the mechanism that secures the feast.

03of six

The transmissible cancer

Animal fact

Devil Facial Tumor Disease (DFTD) is one of the only known transmissible cancers in nature, transmitted between individuals through biting during feeding and mating — first documented by Pearse and Swift (2006). The behavior most characteristic of the species is the exact vector of the disease threatening its survival.

Our reading

The most characteristic thing you do is the thing that has cost you. The strength and the wound share the same address. This is not a pattern to eliminate. It is one to understand well enough to work around.

04of six

Forty embryos, four survivors

Animal fact

Female Tasmanian devils give birth to up to forty embryos simultaneously, but only four nipples are available in the pouch. The first four to arrive survive; the rest do not. The litter is a competition built into the anatomy.

Our reading

You have a clear sense of what gets to stay and what does not. This does not make you cold — it makes you honest about capacity. The ones who made it to the pouch earned it. You know exactly who those people are.

05of six

Island range only

Animal fact

Tasmanian devils are endemic to Tasmania and have been absent from the Australian mainland for approximately 3,000 years, likely driven out by competition and predation. They have not dispersed; their entire population and all its consequences are contained on a single island.

Our reading

Your range is specific. You know this ground, these people, these consequences. You do not expand casually into new territory. What you have chosen, you hold — and everything that happens here, happens here.

06of six

Communal carcass

Animal fact

Multiple Tasmanian devils will feed simultaneously at a single large carcass, using vocalizations rather than fighting to establish access — the screaming competition resolves most disputes without physical injury, despite appearances.

Our reading

The feast is loud but it is shared. What looks like conflict from outside is usually the mechanism by which the group figures out who gets what. You are not trying to exclude anyone — you are being honest about where you stand.

The Tasmanian Devil dossier

The Dossier

Zodi Animal
No. 095 / 144

☼ Tasmanian Devil · Scorpio × Dog

The Bone Feast

Screams at what frightens others away. Bites through bone. Stays on the island.

Profile

Archetype
The Bone Feast
Central gift
Holding without flinching
Central shadow
Strength as wound vector
Protective instinct
Staying where you belong
Growth lesson
Knowing when the bite harms
Power phase
New Moon
Element
Earth
Ideal habitat
Contained, rooted, with clear boundaries

These five traits map where this nature does its most essential work, and where the same nature turns inward. Test yourself against them.

Take the personality test

Tap a trait to see it in yourself

    Tasmanian DevilPick a trait above to see how strongly it tends to show up in you, and which way it leans.

    Two zodiacs, one animal

    Where Scorpio and the Dog meet

    Scorpio brings

    • Carries the full weight of what it knows, including what would be easier to put down
    • Does not release a commitment simply because it has become costly
    • Connects at depth or not at all — few relationships, but entire

    The Dog brings

    • Stays with its people and its territory without requiring reasons to continue
    • Measures the world through loyalty as a physical practice rather than a feeling
    • Keeps faith with the place and the pack even when the pack has become complicated

    What the crossing makes

    The Tasmanian Devil in full function is a creature of complete presence: Scorpio's depth of commitment crossed with the Dog's unrevised loyalty produces someone who stays on the island through all of it — who shows up at the difficult carcass, makes noise about what is there, and does not leave until the bone is finished. You are built for the long haul in a specific place with specific people. The island is the strength.

    Where they pull against each other

    Scorpio holds everything — the knowledge, the wound, the cost — without distributing it. The Dog does not revise its attachments. Together they produce a pattern of staying past the point of safety: remaining committed to the place and the people even when the place is where the damage travels. The whole reading is about knowing the difference between the rootedness that sustains you and the rootedness that becomes the vector.

    Drag the dial to weigh your two halves and watch the blend change

    Scorpio
    Dog
    50 Scorpio In balance Dog 50

    Scorpio holds the depth; the Dog holds the loyalty. At this balance, you stay on the island because you mean to — not because you cannot leave, but because leaving is not the answer to what is happening. The commitment is chosen.

    Your element

    土

    Your element is Earth

    Earth's archetype: The Sovereign · in the Tasmanian Devil: The Bounded Ground

    Earth's archetype in this system is the Sovereign. In the Tasmanian Devil it becomes something more specific: the sovereign of a single island, holding the full weight of every commitment made on this particular ground. Earth at its best is the ground that sustains — the contained depth that makes everything else possible. In excess it becomes the ground that cannot be left: the island that was home becomes the enclosure, and the rootedness becomes an inability to step back even when stepping back would allow the ground to recover.

    How this was calculated

    Every Zodi Animal takes its element from the fixed element of its Chinese year-animal: for the Dog, that is Earth. Scorpio adds Water as a second note. In the five-phase cycle, Earth banks Water — the Dog's groundedness contains and gives direction to Scorpio's depth. This makes the Tasmanian Devil a creature where the depth has boundaries: the island's shores are the edge of the Water, and everything the Scorpio depth finds flows within those shores. Fire feeds Earth in the cycle; Wood checks it. The pairing of animal and element archetype is Zodi Animal's own reading of these traditional systems, offered for reflection.

    DirectionCenter
    SeasonLate Summer
    VirtueFaithfulness
    ColorYellow

    The elements around you

    Wu Xing 五行, the Five Phases, is a system of relationships, not isolated traits. Here is how the other four move around yours.

    Generates you

    火Fire becomes Earth — heat produces ground

    In the five-phase cycle, Fire reduces to Earth; what burns leaves ash and mineral. For a person built like the Tasmanian Devil, Fire types — direct, passionate, fast-moving — are the natural complement. Their energy produces the material your groundedness requires. They heat the carcass so the jaw can consume it.

    You generate

    金Earth becomes Metal — ground crystallizes into edge

    Earth generates Metal in the five-phase cycle; the substance of the ground condenses into precision. When the Tasmanian Devil's rootedness and depth reach someone who can use structure, they receive the earth's yield: the precision that only comes from deep ground. Your staying becomes someone else's edge.

    Restrains you

    木Wood checks Earth — roots break the ground apart

    Wood restrains Earth in the five-phase cycle; the root network breaks through the surface from below. For the Tasmanian Devil, Wood types — expansive, growing, reaching outward — press against the island's edge. This is useful friction: what grows into your containment shows you where the boundary is.

    You restrain

    水Earth banks Water — the island gives depth its shore

    Earth contains Water in the five-phase cycle; the ground is what gives the river its direction. For this animal, the Earth element is what makes Scorpio's Water navigable: the depth has a shore. Without Earth, the Water runs without direction; with it, the depth finds its course. Your groundedness is what the depth runs against.

    Go deeper: Bazi 八字

    This is Wu Xing 五行, and it runs deeper

    This Earth element reading comes from your year of birth — the Dog's fixed element. In Bazi, the Four Pillars, that is only one of four pillars. Your hour, day, and month pillars each carry their own element, and together they specify the ground you are actually standing on.

    The Four Pillars will tell you which Earth you are — whether it is the mountain, the clay, or the dry riverbed — and how the other elements in your chart feed or stress it. A chart with Fire in the day pillar finds the groundedness naturally warm. A chart with too much Water and no Fire may find the island feels flooded.

    BaziRead your Four Pillars ElementsSee all five phases

    Go deeper: Chakras

    Earth in the body

    Held beside the yogic map, Earth finds its closest match in the Root chakra, Muladhara. It governs the sense of ground, belonging, and safety in the body — the thing the Tasmanian Devil has in its island, and the thing it loses when the island is threatened. Its bija sound is LAM; its element there is earth.

    Treat the pairing as an interpretive bridge between two traditions, not an equivalence.

    Root chakraMuladharaBija: LAMElement: earth
    ChakraVisit your Root chakra PracticeYoga for your element

    Take it further

    土ElementThe Earth element in full OverviewAll five elements Bazi · 八字Your Four Pillars Feng shuiSet up your workspace

    The Primal Mirror

    Every reading passes five gates, in order

    One mechanism, five states. Reflex, capacity, defense, distortion, integration.

    Swipe the gates · tap to open each one

    01 · Reflex

    The scream at the carcass

    When something difficult is available, you go toward it — loudly, completely, without checking first whether others are watching.

    The Tasmanian devil does not hesitate at the carcass. It arrives, locates the feast, and makes noise immediately — announcing its presence, its intention, its right to be there. The scream is not anger. It is the first move of an animal that knows exactly what it came for and intends to have it.

    Your version of this is the instinct to engage with the hard thing before others have decided whether to approach. You go toward difficulty. You stay loud in situations that would make most people quiet. Early in life this reads as aggression; later it reads as the person who said the true thing in the room where everyone else was being careful. The reflex is the same. What grows is your understanding of when the noise serves the feast and when it is producing different consequences.

    02 · Capacity

    The one who stays when others leave

    You are the person who arrives at the hard thing, makes noise about it, and then does the work of consuming it completely — leaving nothing unaddressed.

    When the devil's directness and its rootedness work together — when the island is stable enough for the feast to be shared, and when the commitment is to something that can receive it — it becomes one of the most reliable presences in any system. You show up. You do not manage the difficult thing from a distance. You get close enough to smell what has happened and you do not leave until it is done.

    The gift is not the noise. The noise is the announcement of the gift. What you actually offer is the capacity to stay in a difficult situation longer than most people will, consuming it with the full strength of your jaw, and leaving the ground cleaner than you found it. The people who have received this from you know exactly what it is worth.

    03 · Defense

    The island's edge

    Staying on the island is not fear of the mainland — it is the knowledge that everything worth protecting is here.

    The devil's range has contracted over millennia to a single island. From the mainland's perspective, this looks like loss. From the island's perspective, it is total knowledge: every carcass, every water source, every rival, every potential mate — all of it within the range of a single nose. The devil does not need the mainland. The island is complete.

    You carry a version of this: a fierce specificity about where you belong and who you belong to. It is not difficulty expanding — it is a sense that what you have chosen deserves your full presence, and full presence requires limits. The guard is the island's edge. What it protects is the completeness of the commitment inside it.

    04 · Distortion

    The bite that spreads what it carries

    The most characteristic thing you do is also the place where the wound enters — and staying at the feast longer than is safe does not produce more nourishment.

    What trips it. A situation that requires the full weight of your presence: a conflict, a commitment, a person who is in difficulty. You arrive. You stay. The noise increases.

    What your mind says. I am not leaving. This is mine to handle. I have the jaw for this.

    What you do. You stay at the carcass past the point of nourishment. You go deeper into the commitment even as it becomes the vector for the damage. You consume bone when what was needed was to step back from the feast.

    What it costs. The bite that was your strength becomes the path the disease travels. The staying that was your gift becomes the thing that exhausts you. <b>The island has not changed, but you have been spending from it faster than it can recover</b>.

    In love

    You stay in a dynamic that has become damaging because leaving would mean conceding that the commitment was wrong. The jaw gets tighter. The feast is no longer feeding you. You are still there.

    At work

    You take on the problem no one else will touch, because you have the capacity for it. By the time it is finished, you have consumed more than the work required — including parts of yourself that did not need to be offered.

    With friends

    You show up loudly at every difficulty in a friendship until the friendship itself becomes organized around the difficult thing. The feast is the relationship.

    05 · Integration

    Awakened: the same strength, integrated

    You still go toward the hard thing — and now you know the difference between what feeds the island and what depletes it.

    Nothing changes about the instinct. The Awakened Tasmanian Devil does not become quiet or cautious. It does not stop going toward the difficult thing, stop making noise at the carcass, stop staying longer than others. The jaw is still the jaw. The rootedness is still the rootedness. What changes is the discrimination: you go toward the hard thing when the hard thing is actually nourishment, and you step back from the feast when more biting is not more feeding.

    You stop staying because you cannot leave and start staying because you choose to. The difference is that one recovers the island and the other slowly does not.

    Name the form you are becoming

    Complete the sentence: I stay …

    Beyond the reading · ✦ Purple Star

    Meet the awakened Tasmanian Devil

    The Mirror showed you the awakened form in outline. Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu (紫微斗数), is the oldest and most detailed of the Chinese chart systems, and it maps the Tasmanian Devil you are becoming, not only the one you are.

    Keep reading it, in your dashboard

    A free account saves your chart and brings a new reading for your animal each week.

    紫微

    Your full Purple Star chart

    All fourteen stars across your twelve palaces, cast for your Scorpio Dog crossing and saved to your dashboard.

     ☼

    The awakened Tasmanian Devil, tracked

    Your path from the fallen form to the awakened one, followed over time, with a fresh reading for your animal every week.
    縁

    The karmic fortune of five

    Invite five friends to find their Zodi Animal, and your Zodi Karma opens the Circle reading: how the six of you move together.

    Create your free account

    Free. No card. Your chart, your weekly reading, and your invited circle, kept in one place.

    Career and working life

    Your working nature

    An application of the archetype, not a verdict: you are likely to work best when the work requires full presence at a difficult problem, and when depth of commitment is valued alongside output.

    01 / 05

    The one at the carcass

    You lead by showing up first and loudest at the thing nobody else wanted to touch. You do not manage the difficult problem from a safe distance — you get in it, name it precisely, and consume it with a thoroughness that others find alarming until the thing is done. Then they are glad someone did it.

    The risk: the noise of your arrival becomes the story instead of the work. In environments where the delivery of directness matters as much as its content, you may need to calibrate the volume without losing the substance. The correction is not quieter. It is more visible to others what the noise is in service of.

    02 / 05

    Contained, difficult, and worth all of it

    You work best when the problem is real, the commitment is deep, and the work has clear edges. Projects where the difficulty is visible and the full use of your capacity is warranted. Not consulting on surfaces — consuming what has been left behind.

    What drains this animal most: indefinite proximity to something costly without any resolution. The devil needs to finish the carcass. If the problem never gets consumed — if the difficult thing persists without moving — the energy cost compounds without return.

    • Problems that require full commitment over time
    • Environments that value thoroughness over speed
    • Roles with clear ownership of the difficult thing
    • Teams that can handle direct communication

    03 / 05

    The full bite or nothing

    You decide by going all the way into the problem. Half-measures are not your method. When you commit to a decision, it is because you have consumed it thoroughly and you know what it is made of. The practice: tell one person what you decided not to pursue, and why. It shows the discriminating jaw, not just the force of the bite.

    You delegate what does not require your specific depth, and you struggle to delegate the thing that has become yours. What you rarely pass along is the difficult problem — the carcass that only your jaw can handle. The reverse: if every hard problem becomes yours, you become the island's only large carnivore, and that is not sustainable.

    04 / 05

    The feast that does not end

    The loud failure is the moment when the commitment became the point rather than the nourishment. Staying at a project, a role, or a relationship long past its capacity to feed you — because leaving would mean the jaw was wrong to bite in the first place.

    The quiet burnout: not from overwork but from the cost of the transmissible thing. In your case, the exhaustion arrives through the same channels as the strength — the sustained close contact with difficult things, maintained past the threshold where the contact is still feeding. You need periods where the ground is allowed to recover.

    05 / 05

    What compounds for the Bone Feast

    Depth compounds. Every difficult problem you have stayed in past the point where others left, every carcass you have consumed when someone needed it done — these build a fund of competence in hard conditions that most people do not develop because they left earlier. The Tasmanian Devil's long game is the reputation: the one who shows up and does not leave.

    One growth practice: once per month, intentionally leave something unfinished. Not abandon it — step away from it with the intention of returning. Practice the knowledge that the feast will still be there when you come back. You are practicing the difference between the jaw and the island.

    Your Ideal Habitat

    Living with your Zodi Animal

    Contained, dark, and enough.

    The Tasmanian devil lives within a clearly defined island range — its habitat is not vast but it is complete. Every carcass, every rival, every watering point is within the range of its nose. The home for this animal is built the same way: a space that is genuinely enough, where the commitment is to what is inside it, not to expanding beyond its edges.

    Your feng shui palette

    Feng shui builds a room from your element and the one that feeds it. Earth is yours; Fire is what feeds it. Your room does not aspire — it grounds. The warmth that feeds you is the warmth that comes from effort already expended.

    Most of the roomA small accent

    GroundEarthAbout 45% of the room

    Island soil dark

    The deep red-black of Tasmanian soil — heavy, iron-rich, specific to this place. Your ground color is not decorative: it is the ground you stay on. The darkness that makes every carcass on this island yours to find.

    SupportEarthAbout 29% of the room

    Bone shadow

    The colour of old bone on red earth — the trace of what has already been consumed. In the room this becomes the structural material: dark wood, heavy textiles, the weight that makes the space feel like it will not move.

    AccentFireAbout 17% of the room

    Ember coal

    Fire generates Earth — and the ember-red of the devil's ear when it is threatened is the fire that feeds the ground. One warm red-orange element in the room: a lamp, a ceramic, the warmth of effort already spent.

    SparkFireAbout 9% of the room

    Ember edge

    The pale hot edge of the coal before it becomes ash. In the room this is one point of warm light — the lamp at the end of the table, the candle that marks the edge of the space where the work gets done.

    Arrange your whole space with feng shui

    Workspace

    Heavy, functional, and undisguised. The workspace for the Tasmanian Devil is not beautiful for its own sake — it works. Dark surfaces that can take use. Objects chosen for what they do. One strong light source aimed at the work. No clutter, but also no apology for the fact that something difficult is being done here.

    Bedroom

    Dark and completely contained. No ambient light; the room should feel like the island at night — enclosed, private, enough. Heavy bedding. Minimal décor. The bedroom is the den after the feast: the place where you recover from the full weight of your presence, without anything being asked of you.

    Entry

    A clear boundary between outside and inside. The entry should feel like crossing the island's edge — a genuine change of register. One heavy object near the door: a stone, a dense ceramic, something that has weight. The weight is the announcement that this is contained space.

    Living area

    The communal carcass space. Where the feast happens with the people you have chosen. Low seating, centered, with room for multiple people to be in the difficult conversation simultaneously. No hierarchy of furniture — everyone at the same level.

    A recovery corner

    One part of the home where nothing is being consumed. A corner with one chair, no task visible from it, dark enough to be clearly separate from the work. The devil needs time off the carcass. This is the ground you recover on.

    Kitchen

    Central and honest. The kitchen for this animal is not a display — it is where nourishment is produced without ceremony. Heavy materials. Generous surfaces. Food made with the same full-weight attention as everything else.

    Elements to avoid

    • Surfaces that look difficult to use — aesthetics that preclude the full commitment of presence
    • Rooms designed to be left; the Tasmanian Devil needs a room designed for staying
    • Overhead lighting without any warmer source — the pure cold light strips the ground of its weight
    • Spaces with no enclosed corner — this animal needs an edge
    The five-minute reset

    Go to the recovery corner. Sit with nothing demanding your jaw. Breathe the equivalent of the island's night air — not consuming anything, not going toward anything. Five minutes of being on the ground without the feast. The island recovers when nothing is being taken from it.

    土 Feng Shui
    for the
    Tasmanian Devil

    Free with a Zodi Animal account

    Redesign your space with feng shui

    A room-by-room guide tuned to your Earth element: arrange your home so that what grounds you stays in place and what drains you has clear edges. Yours free when you make a Zodi Animal account.

    • Room by room, tuned to your Earth element
    • What to place, what to clear, and where
    • Free the moment you make an account
    Get the free eBook

    Feng shui elements here are symbolic. They support intention and act as visual reminders, not claims that any object, color, or direction produces wealth, health, romance, or success.

    Integration

    Practices for the Bone Feast

    Small, physical, repeatable. One from each room of your life.

    • The lesson

      The Tasmanian devil's jaw is the most complete instrument for consuming what others cannot. What it cannot do is distinguish between the carcass that feeds the island and the one that carries the disease. The lesson for this animal is not about going in less — it is about recognizing when the thing you are biting is still nourishment and when it has become cost. The jaw is not the problem. The problem is applying the full jaw without discrimination.

    • A ritual

      At each New Moon — your power phase — sit with one thing you are currently committed to and ask one question: is this feeding the island or depleting it? No extended analysis. One answer. If the answer is depleting, name it and name one edge you can put around the feast. The New Moon is the darkest night: the one when you can see exactly what is on the ground without anything else reflecting.

    • In your space

      Place one heavy object on your workspace — a stone, a piece of iron, a dense ceramic — that you chose for weight rather than appearance. When you are about to go all the way into a difficult thing, hold it first. The weight is the question: how much of this belongs to you?

    • In one bond

      This week, before you go fully into one difficult conversation, ask the other person where they want you: at the feast with them, or at the edge. Both are things you can offer. Practice making the choice theirs before the jaw decides.

    • At work

      Find the one difficult problem you have been consuming for longest. Name one part of it that does not require your specific jaw — and hand that part to someone else. Practice the discrimination: not every bone on the island is yours to bite.

    Moon rhythm

    Your power moon

    The New Moon is the night when the island is at its darkest — when the devil's acute nose is the primary instrument and the absence of light equalizes every competitor. The darkness removes the advantage of sight and restores the primacy of the close-range read. This is when the full weight of the jaw and the ground it stands on is most effective.

    Your next new moon · Power Moon

    Friday, July 25, 2026

    14 days from today.

    Go to the recovery corner. Reduce the light. Sit with the island — not the carcass, just the ground. The New Moon is the condition under which the full weight of the jaw is most effective. Use it to ask whether the next bite is nourishment or depletion.

    1. Thursday, July 24The night before: do not go into the hard thing. Let the carcass be what it is without consuming it tonight. Notice what you have been carrying without adding to it.
    2. Friday, July 25 · New MoonYour peak: name one thing you have been biting that is no longer feeding the island. Say it once, out loud, in the dark. Then put the jaw down for the night.
    3. Saturday, July 26The day after: make one decision based on the naming. Not a full solution — one edge placed around one thing that has been depleting the island. Let the ground start recovering.
    Explore the Moon hub

    The proverb of your year

    祸huò从cóng口kǒu出chū

    Tap a tile to hear one character

    Literally Misfortune exits through the mouth.

    Meaning What you say carelessly can produce real harm — the bite has consequences.

    For the Tasmanian Devil, the proverb is not a caution about speech. It is a description of the species: the bite that feeds is the same bite that carries the tumor. The mouth is the source of the feast and the vector of the wound. What the proverb asks of you is not to close the jaw — it is to know, before you bite, whether this is the feast or the disease.

    A proverb we tie to the Year of the Dog. The pairing is our own reading, not tradition.

    In relationship

    How the Tasmanian Devil loves

    You know before anyone says so that something in the bond has changed — and you stay at the feast even when the feast has become the difficulty.

    Your care is expressed through presence and persistence. The people you love get your full weight — which means they also get the noise, the directness, and the complete inability to pretend you are not at the carcass when you are. The gap in your bonds is rarely honesty. It is knowing when to put the jaw down.

    How you show love

    Staying. Showing up at the difficult thing your partner is carrying and not leaving until it has been addressed. Being in the feast with them rather than managing it from a distance.

    What makes you feel safe

    A partner who can handle your directness without requiring you to calibrate the volume. Someone who knows the noise is the arrival, not the relationship.

    What they may misunderstand

    The noise as aggression. The loudness of the arrival as attack rather than as presence. The staying that is actually loyalty reading as pressure.

    Your conflict pattern

    You go toward the conflict with full jaw. You consume it completely. You do not understand why others want to leave before it is finished. By the time it is finished, they are exhausted.

    What to practice

    Once per conflict, before you go fully in, say what the feast is: 'I want to work through this. I need to know we are both staying.' Give them the choice to arrive with you before you begin.

    How you show up

    As the one who arrives at the difficult moment loudly, completely, and stays until it is resolved. You do not offer support from a careful distance. You get into the hard thing with them.

    What they may misunderstand

    Your presence at the difficult thing as intrusion. The jaw that is trying to help can feel like intensity when what was needed was quiet company.

    What to practice

    Ask before you arrive at someone else's carcass: 'Do you want me in this with you, or do you need someone to stay at the edge?' Both are things you can offer. Not every feast requires your specific jaw.

    How you show up

    As the one who names the thing the family is organized around not naming. The scream at the carcass that has been sitting in the room for years. Families that run on managed surfaces find this disruptive; families that run on honesty find it essential.

    What they may misunderstand

    Your directness as the problem, rather than the signal that the problem exists. The noise is not creating the carcass. The carcass was already there.

    What to practice

    After you name the thing, wait for one full response before going deeper in. Let the family arrive at the feast before you consume it.

    How you show up

    As the one who took the project no one else wanted and delivered it completely. People who work with you long enough stop assigning you easy problems. You are where the difficult thing goes.

    Your conflict pattern

    You name the conflict directly and expect resolution. The full bite, then it is finished. Colleagues who manage conflict more circuitously read your directness as aggression; you read their circuitousness as avoiding the carcass.

    What to practice

    Before naming a conflict at work, state what you are trying to preserve: 'I want to name what I think is happening because I want the project to get through it.' Frame the feast before the jaw.

    Match the directness. Do not manage them from the edge of the feast — get in it with them, or tell them clearly that this one is theirs. What a Tasmanian Devil cannot use is careful, distanced support. What it needs is another animal at the carcass, or an honest acknowledgment that this carcass belongs to them.

    Test this bond with a second birth date

    Computed across all 143 other crossings

    Matches the wheel favors

    Strongest pull

    Match I · of three

    火

    Moose

    ♉ Taurus × 马 Horse

    1942, 1954, 1966, 1978, 1990, 2002, 2014

    Same trineTheir Fire feeds your Earth

    The Horse shares the Dog's trine, so effort between you compounds. Their Fire feeds your Earth: this one is fuel.

    Read the Moose

    Match II · of three

    木

    Sea Otter

    ♋ Cancer × 兔 Rabbit

    1951, 1963, 1975, 1987, 1999, 2011, 2023

    Secret friendsWood nature

    The Rabbit is the Dog's secret friend, the wheel's quiet allied pair.

    Read the Sea Otter

    Match III · of three

    土

    Cleaner Wrasse

    ♍ Virgo × 狗 Dog

    1946, 1958, 1970, 1982, 1994, 2006, 2018

    Same yearShared Earth

    Two Dog years run on the same clock. Two Earth natures recognize each other on sight.

    Read the Cleaner Wrasse

    Stay at the carcass

    Where to go from the Tasmanian Devil

    Three matches surfaced above; the wheel holds 140 more. Each crossing opens onto others, and these are meanings to explore, not verdicts. Contrast is a mirror for self-knowledge, never a warning.

    Best next reading

    See how Scorpio changes across the other eleven years

    The same depth of commitment, held in eleven different bodies — the fastest way to feel what Scorpio contributes and what the year changes.

    All Scorpio animals

    Animals your instincts trust on sight

    The same quality of complete commitment — creatures that stay, consume fully, and do not manage their presence from a distance.

    Giant SalamanderScorpio · Year of the Ox Honey BadgerLeo · Year of the Dog

    The temperaments that stretch yours

    Lighter presence, wider range, less jaw — the pattern most unlike yours and the one that shows you what the feast looks like from the mainland.

    TarsierScorpio · Year of the Rat

    Same year, other skies

    The Dog's loyalty and groundedness under eleven other suns — each one a different shape of the same instinct to stay.

    Banded MongooseAries · Year of the Dog Moon BearTaurus · Year of the Dog Secretary BirdGemini · Year of the Dog Giant OtterCancer · Year of the Dog

    Scorpio's depth and commitment in eleven other bodies — the same full-weight presence, wearing different build.

    The last chapter

    The Tasmanian Devil is yours.
    Now find out who else feasts where others will not go.

    The Bond Test

    Bring a friend into the reading

    One birthdate is all it takes. Turn the wheel to their year, and their animal steps out to meet the Tasmanian Devil.

    Your first friend

    01 The year they were born
    牛 1995 · Year of the Pig
    02 The month
    03 The day

    The Moon tonightCome back at the New Moon with the one thing that has been depleting the island — and name it. Wander the MenagerieAll 144 crossings. Each one opens onto others.

    The Bone Feast · Scorpio × Dog · Earth 土

    Your line to keep

    I am the Tasmanian Devil: I scream at what frightens others away. I bite through bone. I stay on the island.

    骨

    Where you go next

    Three doors are open. Pick one.

    You know the shape of the Tasmanian Devil now. Here is what the reading did not tell you yet, and where each thread runs.

    Bonds

    Test a friend against your wheel

    Drop any name onto the Tasmanian Devil wheel and see where they sit. Ally, mirror, or friction.

    Fire feeds the Earth that grounds you; Water is what your depth runs against when the island does not give it anywhere else to go.

    Run the Bond Test     Purple Star 紫微

    Your chart is bigger than one animal

    Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu, maps your whole birth chart: fourteen stars across twelve palaces, cast from your exact birth hour.

    The reading here is the outline your Scorpio Dog shares. Your own chart shows which palace runs loudest.

    Cast your chart

    Save your Tasmanian Devil before you go.

    A free account keeps this reading and every animal you test, on any device. Come back to where you left off instead of a blank page.

    About a minute to set up. No card. Delete it anytime.

    Sends it to a friend.

    Tasmanian Devil · 095 / 144 · Earth 土

    Pass it on

    Share your crossing

    Keep every reading you are given.

    Make it permanent

    This device will remember the Tasmanian Devil until it doesn't. A free account keeps your animal, your reflections, and every reading you are given, renewed with each new moon, on any device.

    • Your Tasmanian Devil, saved for good
    • Your identity line and keeper stone, kept
    • The feng shui eBook for your element, free
    Create your free account

    The Tasmanian Devil Test

    Do you take what you need from the hard places, the way the Tasmanian Devil does?

    The Tasmanian Devil is built from five traits. Ten quick questions measure yours against its shape and tell you whether you are a true Tasmanian Devil, or a rare one who carries them differently.

    5 traits 10 questions ≈ 2 minutes No account needed

      Tasmanian Devil You

      How this reading is built

      Each Zodi Animal reading combines four threads: Western zodiac symbolism (your Sun sign), Chinese zodiac symbolism (your year animal), five-element interpretation (Wu Xing, via the year-animal's fixed element), and the real biology and behavior of the animal itself. The archetype that binds them, here The Bone Feast, is Zodi Animal's own framework. Readings are for reflection and self-exploration; they are not clinical assessment, scientific personality testing, or a claim about fate. Animal facts are drawn from zoological references and flagged in the text as Animal fact; interpretive material is flagged as Our reading.

      • IUCN Red List · Sarcophilus harrisii (Tasmanian Devil)
      • Pearse, A.M. & Swift, K. (2006) — Allograft theory: transmission of devil facial tumour disease, Nature
      • Jones, M.E. et al. (2004) — Ecology of the Tasmanian Devil, in Predators with Pouches (CSIRO Publishing)

      Last reviewed 2026-07-11

      One hundred and forty-four animals, born where the Western Sun signs cross the Chinese zodiac years. A slow tradition of meaning, kept under the moon.

      Your reading, at dawn.

      One short passage for your Zodi Animal each morning. The moon's phase, what it favors, and a line meant for you. Free, and it takes ten seconds to join.
